[DONE slice 13] FIX: clicking 'Grant' for Screen Recording CRASHED the daemon (Alex: 'permission request failed: Unexpected EOF' + 'no daemon'). Root cause: CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess requires a window-server connection and SIGABRTs a headless process; the daemon called it. Fix: the daemon NEVER prompts for screen recording — request_screen_recording() returns status only; the GUI shows the prompt itself via CoreGraphics FFI (screen_perm::request() -> CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess; the GUI is a real app, won't crash). Microphone stays in the daemon (requestAccessForMediaType is dispatch-based, headless-safe — verified). Also made the agent's auto-respawn robust: gated on userStoppedDaemon (set by Stop Daemon) instead of weStartedDaemon, so it recovers regardless of how the daemon started (the 'GUI fails to reconnect on restart' report was the daemon being down from the crash; the poll re-discovers fine once it's back; verified kill-9 -> respawn in ~2s). Verified: daemon request('screen_recording') survives (no crash); smoke 68/68; GUI+agent compile clean; re-froze the bundle.

Decisions (2026-06-17, owner): Inno Setup + winget; logon task (not a Service); don't bundle CUDA (CPU int8 / remote openai-compat / Riva / screenshots-only minimal alternatives for non-NVIDIA boxes); unsigned v1 with a signtool hook (CAPTURE_WIN_SIGN_*); cross-platform in-app auto-update (generalize gui/src/update.rs + a PowerShell updater; both .dmg and .exe under one release tag). features.description: Grouped multi-window/multi-app capture picker + microphone selector. The GUI window picker groups windows by application with checkboxes (multi-select across several apps at once); 'Start capture' spawns one /v1/sessions per checked window (per-window screenshots via window_id), deduping app audio per process so only the first checked window of each app records the app audio (macOS audio is per-application, not per-window). A mic selector (GET /v1/audio/mics; devices from the bundled audiocap helper's --list-mics, AVFoundation AVCaptureSession — NO ffmpeg) attaches ONE chosen input device to ONE app via a per-app mic radio; the mic records as a SEPARATE track (mic.s16le / mic_transcript.jsonl), never mixed with app audio. mic_device persists in gui-settings.json. Daemon: StartSessionRequest.mic_device + GET /v1/audio/mics; CaptureSession runs a second AudioCapture(track='mic'); helper gains --mic <id|default> and --list-mics.
acceptance_criteria: The window picker groups windows under per-app headers with a checkbox per window; windows from multiple apps can be checked simultaneously.
Start capture spawns one session per checked window; screenshots are per-window (correct window), and only one session per app process captures app audio.

A mic selector lists input devices from GET /v1/audio/mics and lets the user pick one device + one target app (mic radio); that app's session writes mic.s16le + mic_transcript.jsonl as a separate track.
No ffmpeg dependency: mic capture + device enumeration go through the bundled audiocap helper (AVCaptureSession).
mic_device selection persists across GUI relaunch (gui-settings.json).

features.description: Microphone echo/noise cancellation (without breaking playback). When a mic track is recorded alongside app audio on a device with speakers (a laptop), the built-in mic picks up the speaker output = acoustic echo. First attempt used the macOS voice-processing unit (AVAudioEngine setVoiceProcessingEnabled) which DOES echo-cancel, but it puts the system into a communication audio mode that ducks/mutes other apps' output (user can't hear the captured app) and over-cancels the mic to silence — REVERTED. #37's mic now uses plain AVCaptureSession; headphones are the interim workaround. A proper fix must remove speaker bleed from the mic track WITHOUT affecting playback — e.g. offline/streaming NLMS adaptive echo cancellation using the already-captured app audio (audio.s16le) as the reference signal, or a non-invasive AEC config that doesn't engage system audio ducking. Optionally noise suppression for the mic.
acceptance_criteria: With a mic track + app audio on laptop speakers, the mic transcript contains the user's voice but NOT the app's audio (no echo).
App audio playback is unaffected — the user still hears the captured app normally while recording.
Works for the built-in mic; degrades gracefully (plain capture) if cancellation can't run.

features.description: Session-list UX: SVG icon buttons (Lucide, via a gpui AssetSource in gui/src/assets.rs + assets/icons/*.svg) replacing text/emoji on the session-row actions (folder/clipboard/stop/trash) and the mic radio; a DELETE CONFIRMATION modal (occluding backdrop + centered Cancel/Delete card); and a SESSION PLAYBACK SCREEN (third top-level screen with a Back button). Opening a session shows the latest screenshot + time-synced subtitles; finished captures get a video-style SCRUBBER (draggable timeline + transport: start/-5s/play-pause/+5s/end + mm:ss) that moves the screenshot + active subtitle through time, plus auto-play. GUI reads screenshots/ + transcript.jsonl + mic_transcript.jsonl from disk (ISO timestamps parsed to epoch); no daemon change.
acceptance_criteria: Session-row action buttons and the mic selector render proper SVG icons (no emoji/text labels).
Clicking the delete (trash) icon opens a confirmation modal; Cancel dismisses, Delete removes the session.
Clicking a session opens a Playback screen with a Back button; running sessions show the live screenshot+transcript.
For a finished capture, a scrubber + transport controls move a screenshot and its time-synced subtitle through the timeline; play/pause auto-advances.

features.description: Session pruning + capability status icons. Daemon POST /v1/sessions/{id}/prune {parts}: delete all screenshots, HALVE the screenshot cadence (drop every other frame), and/or remove the audio stream (audio.s16le/mic.s16le). SessionSummary gains capability flags (has_screenshots, has_audio, has_mic, can_retranscribe) updated on prune; the GUI shows status icons per session + prune controls, warning that removing audio disables re-transcribe (#41). Saves disk on big sessions.
acceptance_criteria: A prune action can delete all screenshots, halve the screenshot count (every other frame), or remove the audio stream.
The session summary exposes capability flags reflecting what remains after pruning.
The GUI shows per-session status icons and disables actions that pruning made impossible (e.g. re-transcribe when audio removed).

features.description: Re-transcribe a saved session with a chosen/again model. Daemon POST /v1/sessions/{id}/retranscribe {asr_backend?, model?} reads audio.s16le, re-chunks it (reuse the audio.py windowing), runs the selected ASR backend/model, and rewrites transcript.jsonl/.txt. Gated on the can_retranscribe capability (audio still present). Lets a user upgrade an old transcript with a more powerful Whisper model.
acceptance_criteria: POST retranscribe on a session with audio produces a fresh transcript using the requested model.
Attempting it on an audio-pruned session is rejected (can_retranscribe=false).

features.description: Toggle visual (screenshot) capture on/off. A GUI control to start a capture with screenshots DISABLED (audio-only session) — the daemon already accepts capture_screenshots at start, so this is a small start-option toggle — and, as a follow-on, pause/resume screenshots MID-session (needs a new daemon endpoint to start/stop the Screenshotter on a live session).
acceptance_criteria: A GUI toggle starts a capture with no screenshots (audio + transcript only); the session reflects capture_screenshots=false.
(stretch) Screenshot capture can be paused/resumed on a running session.

features.description: Import an existing audio or video file as a session. Audio: read the file, resample to 16 kHz mono s16le, run ASR → a normal session with transcript.jsonl/.txt (+ the raw audio). Video: ALSO extract periodic frames as screenshots/ and the audio track for transcription, so external recordings get the same session artifacts (playback scrubber, prompts, search). Frame + audio extraction must avoid ffmpeg (the project's no-ffmpeg rule) — use AVFoundation in a Swift helper (AVAssetImageGenerator for frames, AVAssetReader for audio). New POST /v1/sessions/import {path} or capture_import MCP tool + GUI 'Import…' control.
acceptance_criteria: Importing an audio file produces a session with a transcript using the active model.
Importing a video file produces a session with timestamped screenshots (extracted frames) + a transcript of its audio.
No ffmpeg dependency — extraction goes through the bundled helper (AVFoundation).

features.description: Hierarchical multimodal index of a session's screenshots via a REMOTE multimodal LLM (LM Studio on the LAN, 192.168.31.217:1234, Qwen vision model, OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions). Divide-and-conquer over the timeline: build a balanced binary tree by midpoint splits, vision-caption the LEAF frames, then conquer by combining children up to a root summary. Each node stores metadata + RAW artifacts (time range, representative frame, raw vision caption, raw time-aligned transcript slice, fused summary, parent/child links) so the session is readable at any zoom level and re-combinable without re-running vision. Leaf frames chosen by a tunable index sampling rate (0<rate<=1, default 0.5 = every other frame) with a max_leaves backstop; the capture interval is also configurable (down to 0.5s). Transcript fused into the combine step when present. DISABLED BY DEFAULT — enabled only when a working LM Studio endpoint is configured (URL set + reachable preflight); no frames are sent otherwise. Resumable background job + SSE progress (the run is lengthy and the LAN server may drop). Stdlib-only OpenAI-compatible vision client. New POST /v1/sessions/{id}/index + GET .../index + capture_index MCP tool + GUI Index action with a progress tree. Spec: docs/specs/indexing.md (APPROVED — D0–D5 resolved).
acceptance_criteria: Indexing a finished session builds index.json: a balanced binary tree of nodes, each with time range + representative frame + raw vision caption + raw transcript slice + fused summary, and a root whole-session summary.
The run streams progress over SSE (descend/ascend phases, nodes_done/total) and checkpoints so a dropped LAN connection resumes rather than restarts.
Leaf count is bounded by the tunable sampling rate + max_leaves cap; a session without screenshots is rejected (can_index=false).
Indexing is disabled unless a working LM Studio endpoint is configured (index_available); the route 400/503s and the GUI control is hidden otherwise.
Reaches both surfaces: daemon endpoint + Python client + capture_index MCP tool + Rust client + GUI control (parity).

features.description: Transcription quality + settings. Root cause of garbage transcripts (e.g. Russian audio → repeated 'Thank you.'): Whisper hallucinates phantom phrases on SHORT chunks (old 8s default) and on SILENCE (a dead mic looped junk). Fixes: (1) hallucination guards in the mlx + faster-whisper backends (condition_on_previous_text=False, no_speech/logprob/compression thresholds); (2) an RMS silence-gate (asr.is_silent) that skips near-silent chunks in audio.py + retranscribe.py; (3) ASR LANGUAGE as a persisted, on-the-fly SETTING (manager.active_language; POST /v1/asr/language; client; MCP transcription_settings + capture_retranscribe language arg; GUI field in Settings + the live playback pane — a running capture's next chunk picks it up); (4) chunk/sample LENGTH as a persisted SETTING (default 30s, manager.active_chunk_seconds; POST /v1/asr/chunk; GUI chips; StartSessionRequest chunk default None→config; re-transcribe uses the current setting not the old session's 8s). Re-transcribe accepts language + chunk_seconds overrides to fix an existing wrong-language transcript.
acceptance_criteria: A non-English capture transcribes correctly instead of repeated 'Thank you.' (verified: a Russian session re-transcribed at 30s + language=ru produced 219 coherent segments vs 18 'Thank you.').
A silent/dead mic produces no hallucinated text (silence-gated).
Language + chunk length are persisted settings reachable from the daemon, the GUI (Settings + live pane), and MCP (transcription_settings); a language change applies to a running capture on the fly.
Re-transcribe can fix an existing session with a chosen language/chunk length.

features.description: Switch the active microphone on a LIVE capture without restarting. CaptureSession.set_mic_device(device): stop the running mic AudioCapture, start a new one with the new device, appending to mic.s16le / mic_transcript.* so the recording stays continuous (each capture's epoch is real wall-clock, so iso timestamps align). device id / 'default' = on/switch, null/'' = off. AudioCapture gained an append mode. Surfaces (parity): POST /v1/sessions/{id}/mic {device}; DaemonClient.set_mic; MCP capture_set_mic; GUI live Mic chip row in the playback pane (running sessions) that highlights the active mic_device (now in the summary).
acceptance_criteria: On a running capture, switching the mic device changes the recorded input live and appends to the mic track (verified: switch ON created mic.s16le, switch OFF set mic_status=off).
Switching on a non-running session is rejected (400); unknown session 404.
Reaches the daemon, the Python client, the capture_set_mic MCP tool, and the GUI (parity).

features.description: In-app auto-update from GitHub releases, with confirmation. gui/src/update.rs: at startup check() GETs github.com/repos/alex-nax/capture/releases/latest, semver-compares tag_name to the running CARGO_PKG_VERSION, and finds the .dmg asset; Settings shows 'vX · up to date' or 'vY available → Update…'. Update goes through the shared confirm modal (ConfirmKind::Update); ONLY on confirm, download_and_install fetches the notarized dmg and spawns a detached updater script that quits the app + daemon, mounts the dmg, replaces /Applications/Capture.app, strips quarantine, and relaunches. Never installs without confirmation; same Developer-ID signature preserves the TCC grant.
acceptance_criteria: On startup the app checks GitHub and, when a newer release exists, surfaces it in Settings (verified against the real API: 0.2.0 vs v0.2.0 → no offer; the .dmg asset URL is correctly resolved).

features.description: Resolution-adaptive index max_px. Study (daf420, controlled): same frames/prompt, max_px 1024->2048 lifted UE C++ code_fidelity 0.42->0.88, filename accuracy 0.30->0.95, hallucination 0.55->0.10 — resolution is THE lever for systematic small-font OCR (sampling density is not). vision_client.structured_image gains a per-call max_px override; the AUTO path bumps code/terminal leaves to CAPTURE_INDEX_CODE_MAX_PX (default 2048) while classify + non-code stay at the 1024 base; IndexRequest.max_px overrides the client default for a whole build (reproduces the sweep from GUI/MCP).
acceptance_criteria: Auto-path code/terminal frames are extracted at the higher max_px while slides/meeting/video stay at the base (verified: a code frame's structured_image is called with the bumped max_px).
IndexRequest.max_px overrides the client default end-to-end (daemon builds the client with it).
Hermetic + smoke + contract tests stay green; docs/specs/indexing.md updated.

features.description: Enrich the built-in `meeting` extractor with work content. Study (432498 standup): the default meeting schema (participants/active_speaker/shared_content) missed the user's primary interest — task assignments + data. Add task_assignments, data_points, decisions to CONTENT_PROMPTS['meeting'] schema+prompt so auto-classified meetings capture owner->task, ticket refs, dates, and decisions directly (auto already routes real Meet captures to `meeting`).
acceptance_criteria: CONTENT_PROMPTS['meeting'] schema includes task_assignments/data_points/decisions and the prompt asks for them.

features.description: Hybrid OCR-escalation, v1 (flag-now half — auto-re-read deferred per user). _flag_code_reliability post-processes code leaves: sets data.ocr_uncertain where the OCR'd file name is a singleton amid several differently-named code leaves (the local model's confabulation signature; validated to flag exactly the fake names on a real index, leaving real recurring files alone), and attaches data.narration_values (numbers/identifiers spoken in the frame's transcript) so the consumer prefers DICTATED tokens over garbled OCR (1c0c0d finding). AGENTS.md (#57) lists the flagged frames as 're-read these first'. DEFERRED: automated higher-res/frontier re-read of flagged frames at build time.
acceptance_criteria: Detect cross-frame token instability for code content and mark those frames for re-read.
Narration-dictated tokens deterministically win over conflicting OCR.
Does not regress non-code content.

features.description: Index endpoint config as a structured PROVIDER + host:port (not a raw chat URL). Built a provider selector (LM Studio / Ollama / OpenAI / Custom) with per-provider default port + auth (key field only for providers that need one); the daemon composes the endpoint from provider+host+port (a full endpoint URL still works, back-compat). core/providers.py is the shared source of truth; GET /v1/index/providers; GUI Settings persists provider/host/port/key to gui-settings.json (legacy index_url migrated). GUI<->MCP parity via capture_index(provider/host/port).
acceptance_criteria: Settings accepts host:port; daemon composes the endpoint path; existing full-URL config still works (back-compat).
No provider selector yet, but the model is structured so one can be added.
Parity: same config reachable from MCP/daemon.

features.description: Model selector as a DROPDOWN of the provider's available models. GET /v1/index/models lists the provider's /v1/models (providers.list_models); the GUI populates a dropdown (refreshed on provider/host/port change or a Refresh chip) and persists the choice; MCP index_models tool for parity; graceful empty list + reachable:false when unreachable.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon route lists models from the configured provider; GUI shows a dropdown populated from it.
Reachable from MCP/daemon (parity).
Graceful when the endpoint is unreachable (empty list + hint).

features.description: Start-capture PRESET picker. On Start, show a popup of capture presets so the capture meets indexing requirements: e.g. 'Meeting' -> enable mic + screenshots + index preset=meeting (+ task fields from #50); 'Coding tutorial' -> high max_px (#49) + code-aware prompt; 'Lecture', 'General'. The preset wires both capture-time config (mic/screenshots) and the index defaults for that session. Parity with MCP capture_start.
acceptance_criteria: Start opens a preset popup; choosing a preset sets the matching capture + index config (e.g. meeting toggles mic on).
The chosen preset is recorded on the session and used as the index default.
MCP capture_start accepts the same preset for parity.

features.description: Live/online incremental indexing during capture. core/live_index.py builds the index leaf-by-leaf via a binary MERGE-TREE (append → O(log n) new combines, never recomputes; validated 2N-1 nodes/single root across sizes). A daemon worker (start_live_index, gated on a reachable index endpoint remembered from the GUI's /v1/index/status probe) drives it off the capture hot path, checkpoints partial index.json+AGENTS.md mid-capture, and finalizes on stop; no endpoint → the existing post-capture build_index. SSE live_index/live_index_done. Integration-tested: worker captions frames incrementally + finalizes a valid tree.
acceptance_criteria: With an endpoint configured, a live session accrues index nodes during capture (not only after stop).
No endpoint -> unchanged post-capture behavior.
Live indexing runs off the capture/daemon hot path; SSE reports progress; GUI<->MCP parity.

features.description: Fold the 6-eval research into the BUILT-IN classifier + extractors (was only partly done via #49/#50). CLASSIFY_PROMPT now classifies by the CONTENT shown, not the window chrome — the #1 recurring finding was that every screen-recorded YouTube/Twitch capture mis-routed to `video` and lost all code/algorithm detail; now a recording OF an IDE is `coding`, OF a tutorial is `lecture`, OF a call is `meeting`, and `video` is reserved for entertainment media. The `coding` extractor gained a verbatim `code` field (legible now that #49 extracts code frames at 2048) and `lecture` gained `code`+`formulas`.
acceptance_criteria: A screen-recorded coding tutorial classifies as coding/lecture (not video) on the auto path.
coding/lecture extractors return verbatim code (+ formulas for lecture).
Hermetic/smoke/contracts green; indexing spec updated.

features.description: Generate a per-capture AGENTS.md after indexing — a trust-calibration + usage guide for the agent that later receives the capture artifact. The eval study proved the local 9B confabulates verbatim code (invented class names on UE5, fake fns on Strudel), so structured `data` fields (esp. `code`) are SCAFFOLDING not ground truth. The AGENTS.md (written by build_index into <session>/) states: what the capture is (root summary, duration, content-type mix); the artifact map (index.json tree, transcript.jsonl, screenshots/, index_prompts.json); and content-aware reliability rules grounded in the research — verbatim `code` is OCR-hallucination-prone (cross-check the transcript, RE-READ the high-res frame at repr_frame.path for exact tokens); narration is reliable and DICTATED values beat OCR; cross-frame disagreement flags unreliable regions; small-font shared-board text (ticket IDs) may be misread. Makes the cheap verbatim extraction safe to consume.
acceptance_criteria: build_index writes <session>/AGENTS.md with the artifact map + reliability rules.
The guidance is content-aware (code captures emphasize verbatim-code caution + re-read; meetings emphasize transcript-authoritative).
A downstream agent reading only AGENTS.md + index.json knows which fields to trust vs verify.

features.description: User-chosen ASR runtimes via installable runtime packs (owner directives 2026-06-17). NO engine bundled by default; the user picks a runtime matching their hardware (faster-cpu / faster-cuda NVIDIA / remote OpenAI-compat+Riva; AMD/Intel via whisper.cpp-Vulkan or ONNX-DirectML DEFERRED as registry slots — 'option 3 track'), the app downloads a prebuilt ABI-matched pack into %LOCALAPPDATA%/Capture/runtimes/<id>, core/asr/runtimes.activate() prepends it to sys.path + adds its DLL dirs so the FROZEN daemon imports the engine, then the user picks a compatible model and downloads it. NO silent fallback: device is explicit per runtime; load failures surface (audio_status / GET /v1/asr/backend), never a quiet CPU degrade. KEYSTONE VALIDATED 2026-06-17: a lean frozen daemon (faster-whisper excluded) imported an external CPU pack (ctranslate2 4.8 + faster-whisper 1.2.1, 286MB) via sys.path -> 'faster-whisper OK'. Decisions: frozen daemon + packs (not an embeddable venv); NVIDIA+CPU+remote now, AMD deferred. SPEC: docs/specs/asr-runtimes.md. [2026-06-17] LANDED: core/asr/runtimes.py (registry + activate/install/set_active/status_payload/backend_report); daemon routes GET /v1/asr/runtimes, POST /v1/asr/runtimes/install (background+SSE), POST /v1/asr/runtime, GET /v1/asr/backend (+ DaemonClient methods); NO-silent-fallback wired (FasterWhisper device from the runtime, raises on load failure -> GET /v1/asr/backend). Verified: install(local pack)->set_active->backend reports faster-cpu/cpu; routes live over HTTP; smoke 67/67, contracts 4/4. [2026-06-17 b] ALSO LANDED: packaging/build_runtime_packs.ps1 (builds dist/runtime-<id>-<pytag>.zip — faster-cpu = 86MB zip verified); build_windows.ps1 now freezes LEAN (no engine bundled); GUI runtime picker (Settings -> Voice recognition: list runtimes + GPU hint -> Install w/ SSE progress -> Use -> runtime-aware model picker; daemon.rs AsrRuntimes + 3 methods); pack_url() defaults to the GitHub release assets for the version. GUI builds + renders (RENDERER_OK). Remaining for passes:true: HOST the packs as release assets (release flow) + a release build/install verify; AMD runtimes (whisper.cpp/ONNX). [v3 Windows: whisper-cpu runtime pack built + verified end-to-end on the Windows box 2026-06-20 (see #81); whisper-cuda next.]
acceptance_criteria: A fresh install has no ASR runtime; the GUI shows 'choose a runtime' and capture still records screenshots+logs (audio_status reflects 'no runtime', not silent).
Selecting a runtime downloads+installs its pack; the frozen daemon then imports the engine (runtime_available true) and the model picker shows that runtime's compatible models to download.
A chosen GPU runtime that can't load (e.g. no NVIDIA GPU / missing CUDA) surfaces a clear error and does NOT silently fall back to CPU.

Unify the two disconnected ASR systems behind one abstraction (surfaced 2026-06-18 dogfood). Today the runtime REGISTRY (core/asr/runtimes.py, #58 — faster-cpu/faster-cuda/remote, with `remote` active by DEFAULT) is independent of the BACKEND that actually serves+runs models (the mlx model manager: GET /v1/asr/models, backend_available, active=mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo). They disagree: the registry can report `remote` active while mlx is the engine actually transcribing, so the GUI's runtime-active flag and the model list contradict each other — the Voice section had to STOP gating the model list on the active runtime (commit: always show the model manager when backend_available) to avoid silently hiding the user's local mlx models. v3 capture-asr (#64) must make the AsrBackend trait the SINGLE source of truth: the active runtime OWNS its model catalog + active model, exposed as one coherent /v1/asr surface (runtime => its compatible models => the active model). Fold mlx in as a first-class runtime (not a disconnected default), make the model catalog runtime-derived, and ensure `active runtime` always reflects the engine actually in use — so the GUI can gate the model list on the runtime without ever hiding the real local models.
acceptance_criteria: /v1/asr exposes a single source of truth: the active runtime, its compatible models, and the active model are mutually consistent — no state where a `remote` runtime is 'active' while a local engine actually serves+runs the models.
Switching the runtime switches its model catalog atomically; the active model always belongs to the active runtime; mlx (Apple-silicon) appears as a first-class runtime, not an out-of-band default.
The GUI Voice section can gate the model manager on the active runtime WITHOUT hiding local models, because the active runtime always reflects the engine actually transcribing.

features.description: App-only MCP onboarding — the /capture skill makes app users do an unnecessary source build to get the `capture-mcp` command (surfaced 2026-06-18 by an MCP-client invocation on a fresh project; full report: docs/findings/mcp-onboarding-2026-06-18.md). Root cause is TWO gaps: (a) the app bundle ships NO MCP entry point — Contents/Resources/captured/{captured,audiocap} only ever start the daemon, there is no capture-mcp binary and the daemon HTTP /v1 is not MCP-over-HTTP — and (b) the skill (skills/capture/SKILL.md) documents the `.mcp.json` `command` as coming ONLY from path B (install.sh: git clone + venv + mlx + Swift build) with NO discovery of an existing capture-mcp, so on an app-only machine it defaults to the most expensive action and the user must reverse-engineer the bundle. FIX, two halves: APP — ship an MCP stdio entry in the bundle so app users get a `command` with no build: add a `captured mcp` (alias `stdio`) subcommand to the frozen daemon (capture_mcp.daemon.server:main + packaging/build/captured.spec) that runs the FastMCP stdio server (capture_mcp.server:main) daemon-first (bridge to a running daemon, else start one); the daemon binary already bundles click and the server module is already in the package. (Alt: freeze a second capture-mcp EXE next to captured in Resources/captured/.) Optionally have the app write/merge .mcp.json (it already installs this skill + detects Claude Code/Codex) or add a GUI 'Copy MCP command path'. SKILL — add a discovery step BEFORE any install that resolves the command in order [bundled `…/Capture.app/Contents/Resources/captured/captured mcp` -> `command -v capture-mcp` -> known venvs ~/capture/.venv/bin, ~/.capture-mcp/.venv/bin, and ~/.capture/config.env CAPTURE_REPO/.venv/bin] and VERIFIES it answers an MCP initialize+tools/list handshake before writing .mcp.json; demote install.sh to an explicit last resort (state up front it is a network clone+build, ask first); rewrite the misleading 'its capture-mcp command comes from B' sentence to document where the command lives per setup. configure_mcp.py — support a command+args form (to register `captured mcp`) + an --app/auto-detect mode that fills the bundled path. Keep GUI<->MCP parity intact.
acceptance_criteria: On a Mac with ONLY Capture.app installed (no repo clone, no .mcp.json), the /capture skill registers a working `capture` MCP server WITHOUT a source build: it discovers a bundled/existing capture-mcp command and verifies it answers an MCP initialize + tools/list before writing .mcp.json.
The app bundle exposes an MCP stdio entry point (e.g. `captured mcp`) that runs the FastMCP server daemon-first (attaches to the running daemon if present); tools/list returns the capture tool set.

The skill does NOT clone or build the project (refined 2026-06-20): install.sh/install.ps1 were REMOVED from the skill. App users get the bundled capture-mcp; a dev checkout exposes target/release/capture-mcp (built by the dev); discovery finds either. If neither exists, the skill points to the app DMG, never a build.

features.description: Honest ASR model load/warm progress (deferred to the v3 ASR work; requested 2026-06-18 after the 'switching…' placeholder was rejected for a real percentage). INVESTIGATION FOUND: set_active_model is a fast CONFIG WRITE (manager.set_active_model just persists the repo — instant for every size), so a model SWITCH has no work to measure. The actually-slow part is the LAZY first load: mlx_whisper.transcribe (whisper_local.py) downloads (if needed) + loads weights + compiles the graph on FIRST USE, i.e. at the first transcription after a capture starts — so a cold/large model makes the first transcript appear seconds late with zero feedback, and there is no honest 'switch %' to show (we removed the interim 'switching…' text). PLAN: in the v3 AsrBackend (#64) — unified per #77 — make activation (and/or capture-start) EAGERLY load + warm the model and emit a load lifecycle over the existing /v1/events SSE: `download {fraction}` (real % while fetching weights — already exists for explicit Download) -> `loading` -> `warming` (a tiny dummy transcribe to force graph compile) -> `ready` | `error`, plus a per-model load status in GET /v1/asr/models. GUI then shows a real % bar during download, then a LABELED loading…/warming… indicator until ready, then 'active'; the row flips to active only when the engine can actually transcribe, and capture-start no longer silently delays the first transcript. NO fabricated percentages: stages with no measurable fraction render as labeled stages, never a fixed/fake bar.
acceptance_criteria: Activating (or first-using) a COLD large model surfaces real download % while fetching weights, then a clear loading/warming indicator; the model row reads 'active' only once the engine can actually transcribe with it.
Starting a capture with a cold model does NOT silently delay the first transcript with no feedback — the load/warm is visible.
No fabricated percentages anywhere: a stage with no measurable fraction (load/compile) renders as a labeled stage (loading…/warming…), not a fixed-position bar pretending to be progress.

features.description: Remote ASR backends for the v3 `remote` runtime (DEFERRED by owner 2026-06-18 — tracked separately from #64 piece 2b, which shipped the local whisper.cpp path + the GGML model manager). Port the two Python remote adapters into the v3 AsrBackend seam so the `remote` runtime actually transcribes: (a) core/asr/openai_compat.py — OpenAI-compatible /v1/audio/transcriptions, each PCM chunk wrapped as an in-memory WAV and POSTed as multipart/form-data with response_format=verbose_json for per-segment timestamps (env: CAPTURE_OPENAI_ASR_URL [enables it] / _MODEL / _KEY / _LANGUAGE / _TIMEOUT); works against faster-whisper-server/speaches, whisper.cpp server, vLLM, NVIDIA NIMs, or api.openai.com; (b) core/asr/nemotron.py — NVIDIA Riva/Nemotron gRPC (env: CAPTURE_RIVA_SERVER / _API_KEY / _FUNCTION_ID / _LANG / _MODEL). Plugs into the EXISTING seam: capture-asr's `remote` registry entry + AsrRuntimeManager::backend(), which currently returns the stub "the remote ASR backend isn't implemented yet"; remote_configured() already keys off CAPTURE_OPENAI_ASR_URL and the daemon's /v1/asr/* routes already surface `remote` availability. KEY CONSTRAINT: do NOT pull HTTP/gRPC into the capture-asr crate — it is deliberately HTTP-free so the engine cdylib that links it (libcapture_asr_whisper) stays lean (reqwest lives only in the daemon today). Implement the remote backend in a separate crate (e.g. capture-asr-remote) or daemon-side, and have the capture loop / manager pick remote-vs-local without dragging an HTTP/gRPC client into the cdylib (verify with `cargo tree`). Unblocks the local-whisper.cpp vs NVIDIA-Nemotron ASR benchmark (relates to #23).
acceptance_criteria: With CAPTURE_OPENAI_ASR_URL set, the `remote` runtime transcribes a real clip through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint via the same AsrBackend seam (segments with timestamps); AsrRuntimeManager::backend() returns the working remote backend, no longer the stub.
The Riva/Nemotron path transcribes against a CAPTURE_RIVA_SERVER endpoint.
capture-asr (and therefore the engine cdylib) gains NO HTTP/gRPC dependency — confirmed by `cargo tree -i reqwest` (and any gRPC crate) not listing capture-asr / capture-asr-whisper.
GET /v1/asr/runtimes and /v1/asr/backend report `remote` as available when an endpoint is configured, and unavailable (with a clear reason) when it is not.

features.description: Unbundle ASR engines — ship runtime PACKS separately from the app, downloaded through the UI, hosted as GitHub releases with their own auto-update (independent of the app's #48). Realizes docs/specs/asr-runtimes.md on v3/macOS: the app bundle ships NO engine cdylib; ASR is OFF until the user installs a hardware-matched runtime pack (the engine cdylib + any deps) AND downloads a model. A pack is a versioned GitHub-release asset; POST /v1/asr/runtimes/install downloads + unpacks it into ~/.capture/runtimes/<id>/ (SSE progress) where engine_dir() resolves it; a pack-version check (mirroring gui/src/update.rs semver-vs-tag) surfaces + applies newer packs. The existing whisper.cpp/Metal engine becomes the first downloadable pack (build_macos_dmg.sh stops bundling libcapture_asr_whisper.dylib). Includes the pack build+publish tooling (each runtime's cdylib + deps -> a release asset). Owner decision 2026-06-19. [WINDOWS CPU PACK LANDED 2026-06-20 (branch v3-windows): whisper-cpu engine cdylib builds on Windows (capture_asr_whisper.dll) and the full non-macOS workspace compiles release-clean. Pack-build tooling = packaging/build_runtime_packs.ps1 + packaging/win-build-env.ps1 (MSVC+libclang+ninja). VERIFIED end-to-end via the daemon: engine-less daemon -> POST /v1/asr/runtimes/install {id:whisper-cpu, source:<local .dll>} -> installed+active, set model tiny.en -> backend available (whisper.cpp/cpu); transcribe example ran a real 19.6s clip at 74.6x realtime. REMAINING: whisper-cuda pack (CUDA toolkit + cuda feature + archive-extract install), and publishing the packs as GitHub releases.] WINDOWS CUDA PACK LANDED + VERIFIED 2026-06-20: whisper-cuda engine cdylib builds with the new `cuda` cargo feature on capture-asr-whisper (CUDA 13.3 + whisper.cpp), registry gained a `whisper-cuda` (NVIDIA) entry on Windows, the daemon install route handles .tar.gz archive packs (download->.incoming staging->promote engine-dylib-last, interruption-safe; flate2+tar), and DynamicEngine loads with LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_DLL_LOAD_DIR so the pack's sibling cuBLAS/cudart DLLs resolve. End-to-end verified via the daemon: install whisper-cuda from a local 391MB tar.gz -> 4 DLLs promoted -> transcribe on the RTX 4070 Ti SUPER at ~205x realtime (using CUDA0 backend). REMAINING: publish the packs as pack-whisper-{cpu,cuda}-v* GitHub releases (owner-gated).

MLX ASR runtime (Apple Silicon) as a downloadable runtime pack. A real `mlx` engine cdylib behind the capture-asr C ABI (the engine-agnostic seam capture-asr-whisper already uses), shipped + installed as a pack via #81 — the Apple-silicon alternative to whisper.cpp/Metal. COST/RISK (owner-aware): there is no turnkey Rust MLX-Whisper binding; mlx-whisper (mel + encoder/decoder + tokenizer + decode loop) is Python, so this is a reimplementation via the mlx-c C API (FFI-able from Rust) or an mlx-rs-based port — high effort. whisper.cpp/Metal already runs 90-135x RT and stays the proven default; MLX is the second runtime that also validates the #81 separate-pack architecture. Owner decision 2026-06-19.

First-run ASR onboarding — a dashboard CALL-TO-ACTION + runtime/model download flow. When no runtime pack OR no model is installed (ASR cannot run), the dashboard shows a prominent, non-blocking CTA that guides the user: choose a hardware-matched runtime -> download its pack (#81, SSE progress) -> pick + download a compatible model -> ready (the CTA dismisses, transcription lights up). Covers the partial states (pack but no model; model but no pack) and never auto-selects (the no-silent-fallback rule). Visuals delegated to Claude design — brief at design/ASR-ONBOARDING-BRIEF.md (matches design/current-design-system.md). Owner decision 2026-06-19.

v3 LIVE/auto indexing — wire the LiveIndex driver into the Rust daemon. The Python->Rust cutover ported capture-index::live (LiveIndex + run_worker, the incremental merge-tree, #55) but never STARTED it, so indexing-during-capture stopped working. On capture start, if a vision endpoint was probed reachable (remembered in AppState.last_index from GET /v1/index/status), spawn run_worker over the session to index frames incrementally off the hot path and finalize on stop (stop_session flips a per-session AtomicBool). Streams `index` (phase "live", soft asymptotic fraction) + `index_done` SSE so the dashboard's indexing row shows it live; on-demand index builds keep their real percent. run_worker gained a progress callback. Restores the v2 start_live_index behavior.

Harden the in-app updater against non-app release tags. `gui/src/update.rs::check()` reads GitHub `/releases/latest` and runs `parse_semver(tag_name)`; with runtime PACKS (#81) now published as releases tagged `pack-<id>-v<semver>`, a pack release that is NOT a prerelease would become GitHub's `latest`, `parse_semver` would fail on the `pack-...` tag, `check()` would return None, and the app would silently STOP offering real app updates (a downgrade/suppression hazard). Currently mitigated only by a WORKAROUND — publishing packs as prereleases so `/releases/latest` (newest non-prerelease) skips them. Real fix: make the updater app-tag-aware — iterate `/releases` (like the daemon's resolve_pack_url) and pick the newest release whose tag matches the APP version namespace (`v<semver>`, explicitly excluding `pack-*` and any future tag prefixes) AND carries an OS-matching asset (.dmg / CaptureSetup*.exe), instead of trusting `/releases/latest`. Then pack releases can be normal (non-prerelease) without affecting app updates; the prerelease workaround becomes belt-and-suspenders. Keep existing installed apps working (server-side tags are the lever they already consume). Owner decision 2026-06-19.
